GSK culls cough program for drug at the heart of $2B Bellus bet after Phase 3 fail
snippet: Camlipixant, which GSK picked up in its $2 billion buyout of Bellus Health in April 2023, “is unlikely to transform patient care,” the pharma said.

title: Insmed moves closer to $6B opportunity as PAH drug shows long-term durability
snippet: Insmed’s inhalable candidate showed “impressive” long-term efficacy in pulmonary arterial hypertension, according to analysts at Guggenheim Partners, with functional and biomarker improvements through one year of follow-up.

title: FDA disavows Makary, Prasad’s papers as lawmaker blasts ‘shoot-from-the-hip’ policymaking
snippet: Journal publications that suggested changes in FDA guidelines do not “communicate FDA policy or interpretation of a regulatory issue,” acting director Kyle Diamantas wrote in a letter to House Energy & Commerce committee ranking member Diana DeGette.

snippet: The search for actionable genetic targets in cancer has evolved substantially over the past decade. title: Remodeling of the tumor microenvironment in muscle-invasive bladder cancer: insights from single-cell and spatial transcriptomics after neoadjuvant immunochemotherapy.

title: Aspartame and asthma: immunomodulatory effects on airway inflammation.
snippet: Asthma is a heterogeneous inflammatory airway disease influenced by genetic and environmental factors, including diet. title: FDA’s first AI-focused cGMP warning letter signals new scrutiny for manufacturers
snippet: The FDAs enforcement action against Purolea Cosmetics Lab offers an early look at how the agency expects AI-generated work to fit within existing quality systems, with experts saying human oversight and quality controls remain essential.

title: Lilly makes up to $3.8B psychedelics play in AtaiBeckley takeover
snippet: With the AtaiBeckley acquisition, Eli Lilly is jumping into psychedelics, a drug class that Jefferies analysts say could herald the “biggest change” for the field of psychiatry.

title: Merck dives into cholesterol with first FDA approval for oral PCSK9 inhibitor
snippet: Merck’s once-daily pill is the first oral PCSK9 inhibitor to hit the market for high cholesterol, beating AstraZeneca in the race to develop more accessible treatment options.

title: Braveheart, Attovia, Vogenx keep IPOs ballooning with plans to compete in cardiometabolic, immuno
snippet: Braveheart Bio is looking to take on Bristol Myers Squibb in hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, while Attovia Therapeutics wants to challenge Sanofi and Regeneron’s Dupixent. Vogenx, meanwhile, is looking to establish its name in the cardiometabolic space.

snippet: In this episode of Denatured, you’ll hear from Lance Alstodt, chairman and CEO at Biorestorative Therapies, and Paul Mieyal, chief business officer at South Rampart Pharma. We explore why pain has remained such a difficult therapeutic area to tackle and one of the biggest unmet needs today, and how non-opioid mechanis...

title: J&J sees Spravato sales surge as psychedelic competitors loom
snippet: Psychedelics, chiefly Compass Pathways' COMP360, are unlikely to make an immediate dent in the sales of Johnson & Johnson’s depression drug Spravato, H.C. Wainwright’s Patrick Trucchio told BioSpace.

title: Spurred by deep cuts and M&A activity, H1 layoffs could oust 14,000+ in biopharma
snippet: Takeda’s cuts will go the deepest, affecting over 4,700 employees. Layoffs tied to M&A activity include BioNTech letting go of around 800 people at CureVac.

title: Epithelial-mesenchymal transition is associated with altered immune composition and cytotoxic function in triple-negative breast cancer.

title: Advanced Olfactory Cancer Detection: When E-Noses Sniff the Skin.
snippet: Cancer detection and diagnosis are rapidly moving beyond imaging and blood tests to even less invasive-and increasingly sophisticated-methods. In this News and Perspectives article, JMIR Correspondent Liam Critchley reports on recent advances in the growing field of olfactory cancer detection.

title: Functional immune profiling translates T cell dynamics into predictive biomarkers for myeloma immunotherapy.
snippet: Bispecific T cell engagers (TCE) targeting BCMA or GPRC5D have improved outcomes in relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma (MM), yet up to 40% of patients are primarily refractory, and predictive biomarkers are scarce. title: Amid $55B US manufacturing push, J&J turns to streamlining its pharma supply chain
snippet: After committing $55 billion last year to bolster U.S. manufacturing capabilities, Johnson & Johnson is making changes to its pharmaceutical supply chain.

title: Kelun’s Merck-partnered ADC notches late-stage NSCLC win alongside Keytruda
snippet: The Phase 3 results are the first direct proof-of-concept for an ADC replacing platinum-based chemo in a first-line standard-of-care regimen for non-small cell lung cancer, according to Leerink Partners. The readout also represents the second late-stage win for the Merck and Kelun-Biotech asset, called sac-TMT.

title: Pfizer scraps Seagen-developed ADC for solid tumors
snippet: Just weeks after Pfizer’s Seagen-acquired antibody-drug conjugate sigvotatug vedotin failed a Phase 3 non-small cell lung cancer study, the big pharma has quietly culled another program being developed by the ADC specialist.

title: AAIC 2026: Anti-tau space begins to clarify as Biogen presents detailed data on antisense drug
snippet: New mid-stage data from Biogen’s Ionis-partnered therapy diranersen served to both validate the tau hypothesis in Alzheimer’s disease and raise questions that could soon be elucidated by anti-tau candidates from Denali Therapeutics, Eisai and Eli Lilly.

title: Biogen validates anti-tau approach at AAIC, psychedelics full speed ahead, ATTR-CM shakeup
snippet: Biogen’s new data, presented at the Alzheimer's Association International Conference, supports a tau-focused approach to the intractable neurodegenerative disease; psychedelics are back in the news with more positive data from Compass Pathways and final guidance from the FDA; and the ATTR-CM space got a major shakeup...

title: AAIC 2026: Lilly keeps door open for maintenance Kisunla after amyloid clearance
snippet: Eli Lilly’s anti-amyloid Alzheimer’s drug is designed to be stopped after patients drop below a certain amyloid level in the brain. However, at the Alzheimer’s Association International Conference on Wednesday, Lilly revealed it is studying the potential of maintenance therapy for patients who need it.

title: Manufacturing issues prompt FDA to reject Hengrui-Elevar filings for third time
snippet: An inspection of a facility making Elevar’s rivoceranib uncovered deficiencies, leading the FDA to reject a combination therapy that includes the drug. The rebuff is the third strike for the partners after the agency issued manufacturing-related denials for the other drug in the combo.

title: Celcuity wins breast cancer nod, securing ‘differentiated foothold’ in market
snippet: Celcuity’s first approved drug, Revtorpyk, is indicated for the second-line treatment of advanced HER2- breast cancer patients without PIK3CA mutations—a key use distinction that sets it apart from other PIK3/AKT blockers in the space, according to Jefferies.

title: Biotech IPOs surge in H1 of 2026, shattering records and doubling last year’s total
snippet: After a sluggish 2025, biotech IPOs have roared back to life. Fueled by resilient stock performances and improving market sentiment, the total number of public debuts so far this year has already eclipsed 2025’s total.

title: Standing out, not blending in: how AI tools can drive impact during job searches
snippet: AI tools can help job seekers write resumes and mass apply to open positions, but those aren’t the only, or best, ways to use them. A career coach shares how she advises clients to make the most of AI during the application and interview process.

title: Biotech’s China problem: An issue of national security or economic value for the US?
snippet: Congressional letters sent to the CEOs of Eli Lilly, Pfizer, Merck, BMS and AbbVie this week voicing concerns about the pharmas' clinical trials in China highlight an ongoing discrepancy in how government and industry think about the rise of the Asian country’s biotech industry.

title: BIO 2026: Cell and gene therapy sector must break the mold to thrive
snippet: If cell and gene therapy makers are going to achieve their mission to improve patients’ lives, the industry must come together to share information across stakeholders, from regulators to manufacturers to payers.

title: At FDA, ‘caretaker mode’ is better than power trip mode
snippet: UniQure’s planned third-quarter submission for its Huntington’s disease gene therapy may be a harbinger of a more flexible FDA under acting commissioner Kyle Diamantas—but how long will it last? And how can companies be sure these positive decisions won’t just be reversed?

title: Lilly just bought renowned regulator Peter Marks a pipeline to play with
snippet: FDA veteran Peter Marks will now shape the future of Eli Lilly’s vaccines work after the buys of Curevo, LimmaTech Biologics and Vaccine Company for up to $3.8 billion total.

title: Case Report: Pulmonary artery biopsy findings in a patient with a BMPR2 variant-associated pulmonary arterial hypertension.
snippet: Mutations in BMPR2 are the most common genetic cause of heritable pulmonary arterial hypertension (PAH). We report a 47-years-old male with a BMPR2 variant (c.246A > G) who presented with dyspnea and recurrent hemoptysis. Right heart catheterization confirmed PAH with a mean PAP of 46 mmHg, PAWP of 3 mmHg, and PVR of 10.75 WU. A pulmonary artery biopsy was....

title: Liver-specific gene therapy based on self-complementary adeno-associated virus for lysosomal acid lipase deficiency.
snippet: Lysosomal acid lipase deficiency is a rare, autosomal-recessive disorder caused by inactivating mutations of the lysosomal acid lipase gene and accumulation of cholesteryl esters and triglycerides in lysosomes. Treatment with recombinant lysosomal acid lipase is effective, but involves safety risks and the production of neutralizing antibodies. In the curre...

title: Research progress on targeted regulatory proteins in the prevention and treatment of atherosclerosis.
snippet: Atherosclerosis (AS) is a chronic inflammatory disease characterized by lipid metabolic dysregulation and vascular cell phenotypic switching. Its progression is governed by intricate interactions between immune and vascular cells within multi-layered molecular networks. This review synthesizes recent evidence on the specific roles of key regulatory proteins...

title: The role of the NLRP3 inflammasome in hypertension-related chronic heart failure and its potential therapeutic targets.
snippet: Hypertension-induced pressure overload is a major driver of chronic heart failure (CHF) and is accompanied by persistent sterile inflammation. However, the upstream mechanisms linking mechanical stress to inflammasome activation across disease stages remain insufficiently defined. To characterize the stage-specific role of the extracellular ATP-P2X7-NLRP3 i...

title: The glioblastoma ecosystem: clonal evolution, heterogeneity, and therapeutic resistance.
publicationName: Frontiers in cell and developmental biology
journalName: Frontiers in cell and developmental biology
snippet: Therapeutic resistance and recurrence represent major clinical challenges in glioblastoma (GBM), driven by profound tumor heterogeneity and continuous clonal evolution under therapeutic pressure. Conventional diagnostic and therapeutic strategies, which rely on static sampling, struggle to effectively address this dynamic ecosystem. This review synthesizes....

title: Spatially resolved immune niches in thyroid cancer: from hot-cold-excluded ecosystems to precision immunotherapy.
snippet: Although the overall prognosis of most thyroid cancers is relatively good, the benefits of immunotherapy in advanced, dedifferentiated, and some special subtypes still show significant heterogeneity. The existing evaluation frameworks based on PD-L1, tumor mutational burden, or conventional transcriptomic signals are insufficient to explain the complex and....
